contact_lookup_status: Named contact Fabian Schmidthäußler was provided in the user-supplied EnBW job description on 2026-04-12.
availability_override: I am available to start from 15 June 2026 and can work more than 15 hours per week, including up to 20 hours per week during the semester in line with my current student visa.
why_company: What caught my attention about EnBW is that this role is very close to the real operational side of customer and process work: special metering concepts, customer requests, optimization logic, workshops, training materials, and important interfaces. I am especially interested in roles where complex operational topics have to be translated into clearer processes and more usable ways of working.
job_description: EnBW is hiring a working student for Kundenschnittstelle Sonderlösungen in Karlsruhe with remote work possible and a start date from 15 June 2026. The role analyzes customer concerns related to special metering concepts and the connected energy world, coordinates closely with topic owners, derives optimization and handling logic together with experts, prepares information sessions and workshops, contributes to new methods, systems, and processes, supports optimization of important interfaces, works in projects related to the connected energy world, creates training materials in a practical and understandable way, and contributes concepts and further development in the outsourcing process.
requirements: Good knowledge of common MS Office applications
Initial experience or interest in energy industry topics, metering concepts, digitalization, or process optimization is an advantage
Team-oriented and comfortable working with people
Independent working style and willingness to take responsibility
Ideally able to work more than 15 hours per week and more outside lecture and exam periods
cv_about_override: Business analysis and workflow professional focused on customer-facing process clarity, documentation, and structured operational improvement in complex digital environments.
cv_summary_override: Experienced business analysis and product professional with a strong foundation in requirements, workflow improvement, stakeholder alignment, documentation, and structured execution. I have worked across eCommerce, LegalTech, healthcare, and digital operations, with a focus on turning complex operational needs into clear processes, practical handoffs, and more reliable delivery. This makes me well suited for working student roles that combine customer-related process analysis, workshop support, training materials, interface optimization, and structured operational improvement.
cover_letter_opening: I wanted to apply for the Werkstudent Kundenschnittstelle Sonderlösungen (w/m/d) role at EnBW because the work itself caught my attention immediately. It sits in the kind of operational environment I work well in: customer-related process topics, optimization logic, workshops, interfaces, and practical materials that help teams work more clearly.
cover_letter_body_one: Across digital products and operations-heavy environments, I have often worked where teams needed clearer requirements, better workflows, and stronger coordination between business, product, and operational stakeholders. That experience made me comfortable with analyzing complex concerns, structuring next steps, and helping different groups move from discussion to practical execution.
cover_letter_body_two: What makes this role especially relevant for me is the mix of process analysis, interface improvement, workshop preparation, and understandable training materials. Through my work across marketplace products, regulated onboarding, and healthcare workflow digitization, I have built experience turning complex operational topics into clearer structures, more usable handoffs, and more practical documentation. That feels closely aligned with the way this EnBW role connects customer concerns, process logic, and the further development of methods and systems.
cover_letter_motivation: I am particularly interested in EnBW because the company combines energy infrastructure, customer relevance, and operational transformation in a way that has direct real-world impact. That is exactly the kind of environment where I believe structured process thinking and careful execution can create visible value.
cover_letter_closing: I am available to start from 15 June 2026 and can work more than 15 hours per week, including up to 20 hours per week during the semester in line with my current student visa. While my German is still improving and not yet at a fully professional level, I work confidently in English at C1 level with IELTS 7.5 and continue to strengthen my German. I would welcome the opportunity to discuss how I can support your team.
